role: Commander-in-Chief of the IRGC (1981–1997); Secretary of the Expediency Discernment Council (1997–2021); Vice President for Economic Affairs (2021–2024)
bio: Mohsen Rezaei was the longest-serving commander of the [Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps), leading it from 1981 to 1997 — a period spanning the [Iran-Iraq War](/events/iran-iraq-war), the [Iran-Contra affair](/events/iran-contra-affair-1985), and the IRGC's transformation from a revolutionary militia into a major military-industrial force. Appointed by [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini) at age 27, he oversaw the IRGC's wartime operations and its expansion into intelligence and economic activities. After leaving the IRGC, he served as Secretary of the [Expediency Discernment Council](/orgs/expediency-discernment-council) under [Akbar Hashemi Rafsanjani](/people/akbar-hashemi-rafsanjani). He ran unsuccessfully for president in 2005, 2009, and 2013. In 2021, he was appointed Vice President for Economic Affairs under [Ebrahim Raisi](/people/ebrahim-raisi). An Argentine court issued an international arrest warrant for him in 2006 in connection with the 1994 AMIA bombing in Buenos Aires. In March 2026, [Mojtaba Khamenei](/people/mojtaba-khamenei) appointed him as military adviser to the Supreme Leader.
editorial_notes: Born 1 September 1954. IRGC commander 1981–1997. EDC secretary 1997–2021. VP Economic Affairs 2021–2024. AMIA arrest warrant 2006. Appointed Mojtaba Khamenei's military adviser March 2026. PhD economics Tehran University 2001.
